title: A dataset recorded during development of an affective brain-computer music interface: testing session
description: This dataset contains EEG recordings from 8 healthy adult participants during real-time control of an affective brain-computer music interface (BCMI). Participants listened to 60-second music clips designed to induce specific emotional states defined by valence and arousal dimensions. The dataset includes continuous EEG data sampled at 1 kHz alongside self-reported affective state ratings, enabling investigation of neural correlates of emotion and the efficacy of real-time music-based emotional state modulation.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded at 1 kHz sampling rate from 8 participants during a music listening task. Each trial consisted of a 60-second music clip: the first 20 seconds targeted an initial affective state (target A), followed by 20 seconds during which the BCMI system identified the participant's current affective state and generated music to transition toward a second target state (target B), with the final 20 seconds targeting the second affective state. Participants provided subjective ratings of valence and arousal following each clip.
